summary: This rate is still far below NYCHA's 95% target, but it has grown more than sixfold since FY22 as NYCHA ramped up mold remediation under the Baez v. NYCHA consent decree and its 2019 HUD Agreement, using tracking tools like its Mold and Leak Scorecard and a new daily operations dashboard (added January 2025) to target backlogged cases. Complex mold cases require multiple skilled trades and repeat visits, which NYCHA says has slowed progress even as staffing and prioritization improved.

sources.label: NYCHA, Fiscal 2025 and Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Reports
NRDC, "Baez v. New York City Housing Authority"
